description: Long-lived cloud access keys are stored as repo secrets, creating a high-value, non-expiring leak target.
body: Failure mode: deploy workflows store static AWS/GCP/Azure keys as repository secrets and export them into the environment, so a single log leak, malicious dependency, or pull_request_target slip exfiltrates credentials that never expire and grant standing cloud access. Detection signals: cloud access-key secrets present; no id-token: write permission; credentials exported to env across many jobs. Do not store long-lived cloud keys when OIDC federation is available. Safer fallback: use OIDC to assume a short-lived role scoped to the repo and ref via id-token: write, delete the stored keys, and verify the trust policy is bound to the exact repo/branch.
